summary: This week in AI news, significant developments include Meta removing a controversial AI feature from Instagram, Apple suing OpenAI for alleged trade secret theft, and OpenAI launching its new family of models with GPT-5.6. Other notable stories involve advancements in open source AI, AI-powered hardware, and the integration of AI in various industries.
insight: The increasing use of AI in different sectors raises important questions about data privacy, security, and the need for transparent AI practices, highlighting the necessity for responsible AI development and deployment.
highlights: Meta removes AI feature from Instagram due to backlash
OpenAI launches GPT-5.6, promising improvements in cybersecurity and performance
Apple sues OpenAI over alleged trade secret theft related to hardware plans

articles.description: How Deutsche Telekom is becoming an AI-native telco with OpenAI-transforming customer service, employee workflows, network operations, and the future of voice.

articles.description: Preclinical trial is testing the feasibility of humanoid robots in surgery.

articles.title: Meta’s new AI chips will begin production in September
articles.description: The company is taking a modular approach to designing these chips, anticipating that their needs will change as AI evolves rapidly by the time the chips are in production.

articles.title: OpenAI is shutting down Atlas, but its AI browser ambitions are still growing
articles.description: OpenAI is sunsetting its AI-powered browser after less than a year. But it's moving some agentic browsing features to its desktop app and a Chrome extension.

articles.description: AI cheating leads to "a failed society," professor says.
